name: B-59 Radio Room
description: A narrow compartment adjacent to the hydroacoustic shack, housing the submarine's communications equipment — most of it silent, the antenna arrays useless at depth. A single emergency receiver is active on the international distress frequency, its speaker emitting a low, continuous static that has not resolved into a signal for days.
atmosphericDetails: The log of received transmissions shows the last confirmed Moscow contact as forty-one hours ago — a routine operational broadcast that contained no crisis guidance.
A hand-lettered card taped above the emergency receiver lists the international surfacing signal frequencies in order of priority.
The static from the emergency receiver has a rhythm to it — not a signal, but the natural modulation of atmospheric interference that a desperate man could almost convince himself was a pattern.
